base_text: An American and an Englishman working together elucidated the structure of DNA almost 50 years ago; more recently, the Human Genome Project linked researchers around the world, from the Baylor College of Medicine in Houston to the Beijing Human Genome Center. Today our industry's researchers hail from African villages and Manhattan high rises; from Munich and Melbourne; from London, Ontario, and London, England; from Scotland and Nova Scotia--New Scotland; from Calcutta and Calgary. But in the beginning, the infrastructure that supported these efforts--intellectual property, venture capital, streamlined technology transfer--was less widely dispersed and the world's brightest biotech researchers clustered in only half a dozen scientific Meccas. Previous technological revolutions have spread around the world. Following in their footsteps, biotechnology's global diaspora seems inevitable, especially since governments are promoting it. But as our science and business emigrate from early strongholds in the United States, Canada and Europe across oceans and borders and into new cultures, international tensions over biotechnology continue to grow. In just the last few years, controversies have rolled over R&D spending priorities, genetic patents, bioprospecting, transgenic agriculture and drug pricing.

Q:

Running flask app automatically after boot does not work correctly

I am setting up Flask project in Raspberry Pi (python 2.7). It runs perfectly when the python script is called manually. However, I am trying to run the script automatically after boot, it cannot run properly. I have tried modifying /etc/profile to run the script after boot;
/etc/profile
# at the end of the profile
#TRIED sudo python /home/pi/project/script.py &
#TRIED sleep 10;sudo python /home/pi/project/script.py &

sudo python /home/pi/project/start_script.py & # it also fails

start_script.py
import os
import time

time.sleep(10)
os.system('sudo python /home/pi/project/script.py &')

I expect running the server without any problem but after boot, it gives me following error,
socket.error: [Errno 98] Address already in use

However, it does not make sense as this should only happen once. Am I missing anything here? A:

I would suggest creating a service for your app, it's not as painful as you would think. This will start after the network is up so you don't need an intermediate script that adds delays. There are other parameters you can add to this file if you don't want to run as root user (the default), or you want to use virtualenv.

base_text:
Q:

Running flask app automatically after boot does not work correctly

I am setting up Flask project in Raspberry Pi (python 2.7). It runs perfectly when the python script is called manually. However, when I configure it to run automatically after boot using rc.local, it fails to start. The Flask app logs show that it is trying to start, but then it immediately exits without serving any requests.

I've checked the permissions of the script, and it seems like the user running the script (root, as rc.local runs as root) has the necessary permissions to access all the files.
final_text:
Q:

Runing flask app automatically after boot does not work correctly

I am setting up Flask project in Raspberry Pi (python 2.7). It runs perfectly when the python script is called manually. However, when I configure it to run automatically after boot using rc.local, it fails to start. The Flask app logs show that it is trying to start, but then it immediately exits without serving any requests.

I've checked the permissions of the script, and it seems like the user running the script (root, as rc.local runs as root) has the necessary permissions to access all the files.

base_text: However, the gradient of oceanic-continental climate (i.e. longitudinal) was also significantly variable during glacial cycles with important biotic consequences. Range-wide phylogeography of the European ground squirrel (EGS) was used to interpret the evolutionary and palaeogeographical history of the species in Europe and to shed light on its glacial-interglacial dynamic. The EGS is a steppe-inhabiting species and the westernmost member of the genus in the Palaearctic region. We have analysed 915 specimens throughout the present natural range by employing mitochondrial DNA sequences (cytochrome b gene) and 12 nuclear microsatellite markers. The reconstructed phylogeography divides the species into two main geographical groups, with deep substructuring within both groups. Bulgaria is the centre of the ancestral area, and it also has the highest genetic diversity within the species.

prompt_text: Multilocus phylogeography of the European ground squirrel: cryptic interglacial refugia of continental climate in Europe. The theory of classical and cryptic Pleistocene refugia is based mainly on historical changes in temperature, and the refugia are usually defined within a latitudinal gradient.
base_text: Multilocus phylogeography of the European ground squirrel: cryptic interglacial refugia of continental climate in Europe. The theory of classical and cryptic Pleistocene refugia is based mainly on historical changes in temperature, and the refugia are usually defined within a latitudinal gradient. However, our study on the European ground squirrel (Spermophilus citellus) suggests a more nuanced picture. By analyzing genetic data from multiple loci across the species' range, we found evidence of previously overlooked refugia in central and eastern Europe. These areas, characterized by continental climate, provided suitable conditions for the ground squirrel during the Pleistocene ice ages, independent of the latitudinal gradient. Our results highlight the importance of considering various climatic factors and their complex interactions in phylogeographic studies. Moreover, the cryptic refugia identified in our research may have served as important sources for postglacial recolonization, contributing to the current genetic diversity of the European ground squirrel. This underscores the need to integrate historical and contemporary climatic data to better understand the evolutionary history and biogeography of species.
final_text: Multilocus phylogeography of the European ground squirel: cryptic interglacial refugia of continental climate in Europe. The theory of classical and cryptic Pleistocene refugia is based mainly on historical changes in temperature, and the refugia are usually defined within a latitudinal gradient. However, our study on the European ground squirrel (Spermophilus citellus) suggests a more nuanced picture. By analyzing genetic data from multiple loci across the species' range, we found evidence of previously overlooked refugia in central and eastern Europe. These areas, characterized by continental climate, provided suitable conditions for the ground squirrel during the Pleistocene ice ages, independent of the latitudinal gradient. Our results highlight the importance of considering various climatic factors and their complex interactions in phylogeographic studies. Moreover, the cryptic refugia identified in our research may have served as important sources for postglacial recolonization, contributing to the current genetic diversity of the European ground squirrel. This underscores the need to integrate historical and contemporary climatic data to better understand the evolutionary history and biogeography of species.

Mr Speaker gives McGaughey a lift with win in Lexington Stakes

Seven starts into his career, Mr Speaker is now a graded stakes winner on two surfaces as the dark bay colt angled off the rail and kicked like the 2-1 race favorite he was to take the Lexington Stakes by 4 lengths before a crowd of 34,933. Mr Speaker, with Jose Lezcano up, scorched his competitors to win Keeneland's Coolmore Lexington Stakes by 4 lengths. He now has graded wins over two surfaces. The victory was particularly sweet for trainer Mike McGaughey, who had been searching for a breakthrough moment with the talented colt. "He ran great," McGaughey said, beaming. "He's a good horse, and he's finally put it all together. Jose gave him a great ride, and I'm just thrilled." Lezcano, who has developed a strong partnership with McGaughey, echoed the trainer's sentiments. "He felt really good today," Lezcano said. "We just waited for the right time to make our move, and he did the rest." The Lexington Stakes, a traditional stepping stone to the Triple Crown, has produced several prominent winners over the years, and Mr Speaker now enters the conversation as a potential contender for the classics.

base_text: The network firewall protects servers, workstations, personal computers, databases, storage devices, and other intranet-connected devices from virulent data, SPAM, and attempts to breech network security. Security schemes using network firewalls generally work well when network traffic is light to moderate. For example, attacks can usually be stopped using intrusion detection software. Later, security staff can manually review firewall log files to assure that proper remedies have been applied, and to gauge the effectiveness of the remedies. However, as network performance increases and security attacks proliferate, a fundamental problem with network firewalls becomes manifest. A firewall may produce over 10 million various messages (i.e., log data) per day. If this data were printed as quickly as it was created, it would consume a ream of paper in less than 5 minutes.

base_text:
1. Field of the Invention
This invention relates generally to computer network security and more particularly to a system and method for parsing, summarizing and reporting log data. 2. Description of the Related Art
Security devices such as network firewalls and routers act as data checkpoints that examine and block messages that do not meet specified device policies and security criteria. These security devices generate voluminous log data that typically records every action taken, every packet examined, and every rule triggered. While the sheer volume of data can be valuable for forensic analysis and intrusion detection, it is overwhelming for security administrators to manually review and analyze. Traditional log management systems often involve aggregating logs from multiple devices into a central repository, but these systems frequently lack sophisticated parsing, summarization, and reporting capabilities.
final_text:
1. Field of the Invention
This invention relates generally to computer network security and more particularly to a system and method for parsing, summarizing and reporting log data. 2. Description of the Related Art
Security devices such as network firewalls and routers act as data checkpoints that examine and block messages that do not meet specified device policies and security criteria. These security devices generate voluminous log data that typically records every action taken, every packet examined, and every rule triggered. While the shere volume of data can be valuable for forensic analysis and intrusion detection, it is overwhelming for security administrators to manually review and analyze. Traditional log management systems often involve aggregating logs from multiple devices into a central repository, but these systems frequently lack sophisticated parsing, summarization, and reporting capabilities.

base_text: Previous technological revolutions have spread around the world. base_text:
James Jay

Sir James Jay (1732–1815) was an American physician and politician. He was brother of John Jay, one of the Founding Fathers of the United States. While initially a supporter of American independence, he later changed his views becoming a Loyalist and went into exile in London after the Treaty of Paris recognised independence. Early life and education

Family history
The Jays were a prominent merchant family in New York City, descended from Huguenots who had come to New York to escape religious persecution in France. In 1685 the Edict of Nantes had been revoked, thereby abolishing the rights of Protestants and confiscating their property. Among those affected was Jay's paternal grandfather, Augustus Jay. He moved from France to New York, where he built a successful merchant empire.

James Jay

Sir James Jay (1732–1815) was an American physician and politician. He was brother of John Jay, one of the Founding Fathers of the United States. Born in Rye, New York, James Jay received his early education at home and later attended King’s College (now Columbia University) in New York City, graduating in 1753. He studied medicine in Edinburgh, Scotland, and returned to New York in 1759 to practice. He quickly established a reputation as a skilled and respected physician.

He studied medicine in Edinburgh, Scotland, and returned to New York in 1759 to practice. He quickly established a reputation as a skilled and respected physician.

base_text: Numerous manufacturers sell grain drills which are utilized extensively throughout the world for planting grains, such as wheat, soybeans, oats, barley, etc. Grain drills are towed by tractors and plant wide swaths, such as 10 or more feet, with a single pass. These machines are designed to distribute seeds evenly and at a consistent depth, ensuring optimal growing conditions. They typically feature individual seed meters or openers that create furrows in the soil, deposit the seeds, and then cover them up. Grain drills can be configured to plant different types of seeds and can be adjusted to accommodate various soil conditions. They are an essential tool in modern agriculture, helping farmers to plant large areas efficiently and effectively. Additionally, some advanced models come equipped with precision technology, such as GPS guidance, to further enhance planting accuracy and productivity.

base_text:
Q:

display: inline-block with alternate flows at low media width

inline-block is the awesomest CSS tag ever. (That mean I only learned how it works last night.)

But now that I've grasped its power, I'm eager to experiment. One idea I have is to create a layout where at lower media widths, the inline-block elements switch to an alternate flow. For instance, I could have three items in a row that, when the screen gets smaller, the third item moves to the top and the remaining two take up the full width. This way, I can optimize my layout for different screen sizes using the versatility of inline-block. The possibilities seem endless!
final_text:

display: inline-block with alternate flows at low media width

infine-block is the awesomest CSS tag ever. (That mean I only learned how it works last night.)

But now that I've grasped its power, I'm eager to experiment. One idea I have is to create a layout where at lower media widths, the inline-block elements switch to an alternate flow. For instance, I could have three items in a row that, when the screenn gets smaller, the third item moves to the top and the remaining two take up the full width. Just before the blog post noted above was published, Krugman tweeted “A few thoughts on the process of passing Trumpcare” that fail to pass the laugh test. Among his “few thoughts,” Krugman included this howler: “ACA advocates were always clear about what they were trying to do and how they were trying to do it.” To avoid using the “O” word again, let’s just call this horse manure. Krugman assumes that his readers have forgotten what Jonathan Gruber, a key architect of Obamacare, told us about the kind of clarity its advocates actually sought:

This bill was written in a tortured way... if you had a law which said that healthy people are going to pay in, you made explicit healthy people pay in and sick people get money, it would not have passed.... Lack of transparency is a huge political advantage. And basically, call it the stupidity of the American voter or whatever, but basically that was really critical for the thing to pass.

base_text:
An American Tragedy (opera)

An American Tragedy is an opera in two acts composed by Tobias Picker, with a libretto by Gene Scheer. This was Picker's fourth opera, written four years after the debut of Thérèse Raquin (also composed with Scheer). The opera premiered on October 12, 2005, at the Metropolitan Opera in New York City. It is based on the 1925 novel of the same name by Theodore Dreiser, which tells the story of Clyde Griffiths, a young man who dreams of wealth and success but finds himself entangled in a web of ambition, love, and tragedy.

Fourteen (48%) had presence of anti-Bb antibodies. The patients had more fatigue and poorer health-related quality of life as compared to normative data, but were not more depressed, anxious, or hypochondriacal.

prompt_text: The phenomenon of 'chronic Lyme'; an observational study. To chart clinical, laboratory, and psychometric profiles in patients who attribute their complaints to chronic Lyme disease.
base_text: The phenomenon of 'chronic Lyme'; an observational study. To chart clinical, laboratory, and psychometric profiles in patients who attribute their complaints to chronic Lyme disease. To this end, we will recruit a cohort of individuals who self-identify as having chronic Lyme disease and conduct a comprehensive assessment of their symptoms, laboratory findings, and psychometric profiles. This will include a detailed medical history, physical examination, and a series of standardized questionnaires to evaluate their quality of life, functional status, and psychological well-being. Additionally, we will perform a thorough review of their laboratory test results, including those for Lyme disease and other relevant markers.
final_text: The phenomenon of 'chronic Lyme'; an observational study. To chart clinical, laboratory, and psychometric profiles in patients who attribute their complaints to chronic Lyme disease. To this end, we will recruit a cohort of individuals who self-identify as having chronic Lyme disease and conduct a comprehensive assessment of their symptoms, laboratory findings, and psychometric profiles. This will include a detailed medical history, physical examination, and a series of standardized questionnaires to evaluate their quality of life, functional status, and psychological well-being. Additionally, we will perform a thorough review of their laboratory test results, including those for Lyme disease and other relevant markers.

final_text: 1. introduxtion { # sec1-molecules-21-00781 } =============== Primary liver cancer is a common malignant tumor, and about 260,000 people die from liver cancer all over the world every year. The prognosis of liver cancer is generally poor, with a 5-year survival rate of than 10 % for patients diagnosed with advanced-stage disease. Hepatocellular carcinoma ( HCC ) accounts for approximately 80 – 90 % of primary liver cancers, while cholangiocarcinoma ( CCA ) and other rare liver cancers constitute the remaining portion. HCC is often associated with chronic liver diseases, such as hepatitis B virus ( HBV ) infection, hepatitis C virus ( HCV ) infection, and cirrhosis.

§ 1111." The "murder cross-reference" resulted in Reyes's having a base offense level of 43, a level mandating a life sentence.3

26
This Circuit's recent decisions have eliminated Reyes's Apprendi prayer for relief. In a similar case, a defendant convicted of participating in a drug conspiracy was sentenced to life imprisonment after the sentencing court held him accountable for the deaths of four people, even though the defendant did not participate directly in the actual killing of the victims and was not charged with murder in the district court. United States v. Newton, 326 F.3d 253, 265 (1st Cir.2003). The sentence was affirmed because "[a] sentencing court may ... consider relevant conduct of the defendant for purposes of making Guidelines determinations, even if he has not been charged with — and indeed, even if he has been acquitted of — that conduct, so long as the conduct can be proved by a preponderance of the evidence."

345 F.3d 1
UNITED STATES of America, Appellee,v.Rubén REYES-ECHEVARRÍA, Defendant, Appellant. No. 02-1653. Argued: July 11, 2003 Decided: August 13, 2003

Before: Baum, Chief Judge, and Selya and Howard, Circuit Judges.

PER CURIAM: Defendant Rubén Reyes-Echevarría appeals his conviction for conspiracy to possess with intent to distribute over five kilograms of cocaine, in violation of 21 U.S.C. § 846. He argues that the district court erred in denying his motion to suppress evidence seized from a vehicle he occupied, as the stop was unlawful and the search was conducted without probable cause or a warrant. We affirm.

The facts of this case are largely undisputed.

New leaf had been planted in the Apemso Kingship. Foes of the King were fast gathering and looking for ways to oust him out. Their sole line of changing minds of elders was “Do you want another man to take over our land?” For over a decade, they had always operated in secrecy, planning on how to overthrow the present power. The whispers had grown louder, bolder even, seeping into the marketplaces and echoing in the royal gardens. It wasn’t enough to simply sow doubt; they needed to cultivate fear. A series of minor incidents, easily dismissed as accidents or misfortune, began to plague the kingdom. A crucial bridge collapsed, delaying trade and causing hardship. A vital well dried up, leaving a village parched and desperate.

The tubular body must be mechanically secured or adhesively bonded to the seal to effect a connection therebetween that can withstand the vibration and heat within an engine compartment. Furthermore, the connection between the tubular body and the molded seal tends to break after the air duct has been repeatedly detached and re-attached to the air filter or other receiving port. And the connection is further stressed by the pressurized air flowing through the duct.
final_text: Although lighter and more compact than a rubber air duct, the two-piece construction has its own inherent problems. The tubular body must be mechanically secured or adhesively bonded to the seal to effect a connection therebetween that can withstand the vibration and heat within an engine compartment. Furthermore, the connection between the tubular body and the molded seal tends to break after the air duct has been repeatedly detached and re-attached to the air filter or other receiving port. And the connection is further stressed by the pressurized air flowing through the duct.

Q:

Casting a linear (in)equality into a linear program problem

Suppose I have the systems $$S_1: Ax \leq b$$ where $A \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times m}$, $x \in \mathbb{R}^m$ and $b \in \mathbb{R}^n$, and $$S_2 : y^{\intercal}A=0,\\ y \geq 0,\\ y^{\intercal}b < 0 $$
where $y \in \mathbb{R}^n$. I want to cast these systems into a linear programming form. How would I do that? Specifically, how should I pick my objective functions? A:

I don't know what the best way to prove this, but obviously this is rephrased version of Farkas Lemma, therefore we can prove it similarly to proof of Farkas Lemma. The primal problem
$max\space0^{T}x$
$S_{1} : Ax \leq b$
$x$ is unrestricted 
The dual problem
$min\space y^{T}b$
$y^{T}A=0$
$y\geq0$
We have equality in constraint because of unrestricted $x$ in the primal problem. When the first condition holds, the primal is feasible and its optimal objective is obviously zero. By applying the weak duality theorem, we have that any dual feasible vector $y$ (that is, one which satisfies $y^{T}A=0$) must have $y^{T}b\geq0$ (the primal solution is $0$ is a lower bound), therefore the conditions $y^{T}A=0$ and $y^{T}b<0$ cannot simultaneously hold, so the second condition is not satisfied.

Q:

Casting a linear (in)equality into a linear program problem

Suppose I have the systems $$S_1: Ax \leq b$$ where $A \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times m}$, $x \in \mathbb{R}^m$ and $b \in \mathbb{R}^n$, and $$S_2 : y^{\intercal}A=0,\\ y \geq 0,\\ y^{\intercal}b < 0 $$
where $y \in \mathbb{R}^n$. I want to cast these systems into a linear programming form. The first system, $S_1$, is already in a form that is easily adaptable to linear programming. We can rewrite $Ax \leq b$ as $-Ax \leq -b$. Let $x' = -x$. Then $Ax' \geq -b$.  We can introduce slack variables $s \geq 0$ such that $Ax' + s = -b$.  This expresses the inequality as an equality with non-negative slack.

Now consider $S_2$. This system represents a separation condition. The variables $y$ are constrained to be non-negative, and $y^{\intercal}A=0$ is a homogeneous constraint.
final_text:

base_text:
Stephanie Silverman of The Belcourt Theatre: FACES of Nashville

As Nashville continues to change, there are few sacred spots that remain constants in the city’s fabric. The Belcourt Theatre is one. Since 1925, this historic building has been a beacon for film enthusiasts and a cornerstone of Nashville's cultural landscape. Stephanie Silverman, the executive director, has been at the helm of this iconic institution for over a decade. Under her leadership, The Belcourt has evolved into a vibrant hub that celebrates the art of film while fostering community engagement. Silverman's passion for cinema and her commitment to preserving the theatre's rich heritage have made her a driving force behind its success. The Belcourt's diverse programming, which includes independent films, classic revivals, and educational initiatives, reflects Silverman's vision of making cinema accessible and enjoyable for all. Her dedication to the theatre and its mission has earned her a respected place among Nashville's cultural leaders.

460 F.Supp. 320 (1978)
Ronald BRADLEY et al., Plaintiffs,
and
LULAC Council No. 33, Defendant.



**MEMORANDUM OPINION AND ORDER**



**I. INTRODUCTION**



This action is a class action brought by plaintiffs Ronald Bradley, James Davis, William Hogue, Jr., and James Johnson, on behalf of themselves and all other Hispanic Americans residing in the Rio Grande Valley of Texas. Plaintiffs allege that the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) engages in discriminatory practices against Hispanic motorists in the form of selective enforcement of traffic laws, unwarranted stops, searches, and seizures, and the imposition of excessive fines and penalties. Plaintiffs seek injunctive relief to prevent DPS from continuing these alleged discriminatory practices, as well as monetary damages.



Defendants, the DPS and its Director, Colonel Hiram M. Rhoden, have moved to dismiss the complaint for lack of subject matter jurisdiction. They contend that plaintiffs have failed to allege a sufficient basis for federal court jurisdiction and that the claims are exclusively state matters.



**II.

Type 1 Diabetes

Type 1 diabetes incidence among children under 18 years of age in Israel increased by 6% per annum between 2000 and 2008. Among children under 5 years of age, it increased by 104% in 6 years.
base_text:
Type 1 Diabetes

Type 1 diabetes incidence among children under 18 years of age in Israel increased by 6% per annum between 2000 and 2008. Among children under 5 years of age, it increased by 104% in 6 years. This alarming rise has prompted extensive research into potential environmental and genetic factors. While a family history of diabetes significantly increases risk, it doesn't explain the observed surge, suggesting a role for modifiable environmental influences. Several hypotheses are being investigated, including the impact of viral infections, dietary factors, and changes in gut microbiota during early childhood. The rapid increase in younger children is particularly concerning, as it often leads to more complex management challenges and potentially poorer long-term outcomes. Public health initiatives are now focused on early detection, prevention strategies, and improved support for families affected by this increasingly prevalent condition.

base_text: Performance of supply-oriented family planning policy in Bangladesh: an examination. Using data from four nationally representative sample surveys conducted since 1968, this paper examines the performance of the family planning program of Bangladesh, which placed emphasis on its supply component. The very low contraceptive prevalence rate and the large gap between knowledge and use of contraceptive methods indicates the poor performance of the program. After discussing the probable reasons for such performance, the paper suggests accelerating the demand for contraceptives among the married women of Bangladesh through persuasion and by improving the existing contraceptive distribution system. This is required for increasing the use of family planning methods in the country.

base_text: Performance of supply-oriented family planning policy in Bangladesh: an examination. Using data from four nationally representative sample surveys conducted since 1968, this paper examines the performance of the family planning program of Bangladesh, which placed emphasis on its supply component. The very low contraceptive prevalence rate and the large gap between knowledge and use of contraceptive methods indicates the poor performance of the program. After discussing the probable reasons for such performance, the paper suggests accelerating the demand for contraceptives among the married women of Bangladesh through persuasion and by improving the existing contraceptive distribution system. The study finds that the supply-oriented approach, while necessary, is not sufficient to achieve significant improvements in contraceptive prevalence. It recommends a shift towards demand-oriented strategies, such as targeted health education campaigns and community-based outreach programs, to complement the existing supply-driven efforts. Additionally, the paper underscores the importance of addressing socio-cultural barriers and empowering women to make informed decisions about their reproductive health. By integrating these demand-side interventions with the ongoing supply initiatives, the family planning program in Bangladesh can enhance its overall effectiveness and contribute more substantially to the improvement of public health and socio-economic conditions.

base_text:
Blafard

A blafard is an imaginary creature purportedly found in the early United States. Blafards, also called "kackerlackes", were "strange albinos long surmised to be the result of some kind of simian crossbreeding", but of an "accidental variety". Blafards were imagined by Europeans, notably Cornelius De Pauw in the 18th century. This creature was a symbol of the degeneration of America in the minds of Europeans, along with the "hermaphrodite", which "epitomized Americans' sexual disorder. Blafards were imagined to be "caused by a deficiency 'in their parents' spermatic liquor'" and were "absolutely deprived of the power of generation, or did not engender children that resemble them". Blafards were a manifestation of the early Anti-Americanism that originated in Europe in the 18th century.

Blafards, also called "kackerlackes", were "strange albinos long surmised to be the result of some kind of simian crossbreeding", but of an "accidental variety". They were said to dwell in the dark corners of the American South, particularly in the swamps and bayous of Louisiana and Florida. Blafards were described as being small, no larger than a house cat, with pale, almost translucent skin and large, glowing eyes. They were said to be nocturnal creatures, only coming out under the cover of darkness to scavenge for food. Some accounts even claimed that they had a penchant for human flesh, making them a terrifying subject of local folklore.

They were said to dwell in the dark corners of the American South, particularly in the swamps and bayous of Louisiana and Florida. Blafards were described as being small, no larger than a house cat, with pale, almost translucent skin and large, glowing eyes. They were said to be nocturnal creatures, only coming out under the cover of darkness to scavenge for food. Some accounts even claimed that they had a penchant for human flesh, making them a terrifying subject of local folklore.

base_text:
Genesis API and custom software

In the past few months I wrote several ATS and custom execution or front end software for serious Genesis traders. The C++ API was the only one available then. It is a very comprehensive API, with little or no documentation and simply required an expert level C++ software designer. The good thing is : it can do about anything, it's very fast, has a gazilion options and features. The bad thing, of course, is that it’s C++. Many potential users were intimidated by the complexity and build process. I’ve been slowly porting functionality and simplifying the interfaces for a wider audience. The initial focus was on high-frequency trading strategies, but I've also built tools for order flow analysis, portfolio management, and even some experimental AI-driven execution. It's been a fascinating journey, really digging deep into the intricacies of the Genesis platform and understanding how it operates at its core. I’ve learned a *lot* about market microstructure, order routing, and the subtle nuances of high-speed trading. And I've also accumulated a substantial collection of debuggers, profilers, and cryptic error messages.

abstract: 'Binary neutron star mergers have been recently confirmed to be the progenitors of the optical transients kilonovae (KNe). KNe are powered by the radioactive decay of neutron-rich elements (r-process elements) which are believed to be the product of disruption of neutron stars during their merger. KNe exhibit interesting parallels with type Ia supernovae (SNe), whose light curves show specific correlations which allow them to be used as standardizable candles. In this paper, we investigate whether KNe light curves could exhibit similar correlations. To this end, we analyze a set of kilonova light curves simulated with a radiative transfer code, and explore potential correlations between their peak luminosities, decline rates, and other parameters. We find that, similar to type Ia SNe, KNe light curves may exhibit correlations that could be exploited for distance measurements and cosmological studies. However, the correlations are less tight and more complex due to the diverse physical processes involved in KNe. We discuss the implications of our findings and the prospects for using KNe as standardizable candles in the era of multi-messenger astronomy.

base_text: In my Contemporary Photographic Concerns class with Chris Fraser, I made a glossary. It is a list of terms and accompanying artworks/ images/biographies which are relevant to my artistic practices. The images were generated by google searching different sets of words relevant to my artistic practice, (impression, distortion, syntax) then selecting the image option. The main guideline is to begin with 10 terms, then do less and less, and then random groupings. This exercise greatly improved my own understanding of my work and strengthened my understanding of my work in relation to a local, national, & global networks of art practices.

base_text: Large-scale anisotropy in stably stratified rotating flows. We present results from direct numerical simulations of the Boussinesq equations in the presence of rotation and/or stratification, both in the vertical direction. The runs are forced isotropically and randomly at small scales and have spatial resolutions of up to 1024(3) grid points and Reynolds numbers of ≈1000. We first show that solutions with negative energy flux and inverse cascades develop in rotating turbulence, whether or not stratification is present. However, the purely stratified case is characterized instead by an early-time, highly anisotropic transfer to large scales with almost zero net isotropic energy flux. This is consistent with previous studies that observed the development of vertically sheared horizontal winds, although only at substantially later times. However, and unlike previous works, when sufficient scale separation is allowed between the forcing scale and the domain size, the kinetic energy displays a perpendicular (horizontal) spectrum with power-law behavior compatible with ∼k(⊥)(-5/3), including in the absence of rotation.
final_text: Large-scale anisotropy in stably stratified rotating flows. We present results from direct numerical simulations of the Boussinesq equations in the presence of rotation and/or stratification, both in the vertical direction. The runs are forced isotropically and randomly at small scales and have spatial resolutions of up to 1024(3) grid points and Reynolds numbers of ≈1000. We first show that solutions with negative energy flux and inverse cascades develop in rotating turbulence, whether or not stratification is present. However, the purely stratified case is characterized instead by an early-time, highly anisotropic transfer to large scales with almost zero net isotropic energy flux. This is consistent with previous studies that observed the development of vertically sheared horizontal winds, although only at substantially later times. However, and unlike preevious works, when sufficient scale separation is allowed betwen the forcing scale and the domain size, the kinetic energy displays a perpendicular (horizontal) spectrum with power-law behavior compatible with ∼k(⊥)(-5/3), including in the absence of rotation.

prompt_text: Large-scale anisotropy in stably stratified rotating flows. We present results from direct numerical simulations of the Boussinesq equations in the presence of rotation and/or stratification, both in the vertical direction.
base_text: Large-scale anisotropy in stably stratified rotating flows. We present results from direct numerical simulations of the Boussinesq equations in the presence of rotation and/or stratification, both in the vertical direction. The simulations are performed on a triply periodic domain with a focus on understanding the emergence and characteristics of large-scale, coherent structures. Our parameter ranges cover a broad spectrum of Richardson numbers (Ri) and Rossby numbers (Ro), allowing us to explore the interplay between buoyancy and Coriolis forces. We observe a transition from small-scale, turbulent dynamics at high Ri and low Ro to a dominance of large-scale, geostrophic-like flows as Ri decreases and Ro increases. A key finding is the development of elongated, columnar structures aligned with the rotation axis, even in the absence of significant vertical stratification. These structures exhibit a distinct anisotropy, with their length scales significantly exceeding their width and height. We quantify this anisotropy using various statistical measures, including aspect ratios and directional alignment tensors. Furthermore, we investigate the energy cascade within these flows, finding evidence for a suppressed inertial range and a direct transfer of energy from the mean flow to the large-scale structures.
final_text: Large-scale anisotropy in stably stratified rotating flows. We present results from direct numerical simulations of the Boussinesq equations in the presence of rotation and/or stratification, both in the vertical direction. The simulations are performed on a triply periodic domain with a focus on understanding the emergence and characteristics of large-sCale, coherent structures. A key finding is the develapment of elongated, columnar structures aligned with the rotation axis, even in the absence of significant vertical stratification. These structures exhibit a distinct anisotropy, with their length scales significantly exceeding their width and height. We quantify this anisotropy using various statistical measures, including aspect ratios and directional alignment tensors. Furthermore, we investigate the energy cascade within these flows, finding evidence for a suppressed inertial range and a direct transfer of energy from the mean flow to the large-scale structures.

Ask HN: Do we really need volume controls on embedded videos? - valgaze Why should a user have to play equalizer on two sets of audio controls (the main computer audio and the audio of the tiny box on the screen. )

Just my $0.02, but I think on/mute gets the job done.

I noticed these folks didn't bother with a volume slider on their embedded video: http://ycombinator.posterous.com/embark-nyc-is-the-1-mta-award-winning-best-ap ====== dtsingletary It's absolutely necessary; it should, in fact, be illegal to omit a volume control on any audio-playing control, embed, device, or otherwise. This is a bit of a tangent, but stay with me: Much like with playing audio without asking for an opt-in (PLAY button), not having a volume control makes assumptions: a) The computer's volume is at a sane level.
base_text:
Ask HN: Do we really need volume controls on embedded videos? - valgaze Why should a user have to play equalizer on two sets of audio controls (the main computer audio and the audio of the tiny box on the screen. )

Just my $0.02, but I think on/mute gets the job done.

I noticed these folks didn't bother with a volume slider on their embedded video: http://ycombinator.posterous.com/embark-nyc-is-the-1-mta-award-winning-best-ap ====== dtsingletary It's absolutely necessary; it should, in fact, be illegal to omit a volume control on any audio-playing control, embed, device, or otherwise. This is a bit of a tangent, but stay with me: Much like with playing audio without asking for an opt-in (PLAY button), not having a volume control makes assumptions: a) The computer's volume is at a sane level. b) The user is prepared for audio to suddenly blare out of their speakers or headphones at an unpredictable volume level. c) The user is in an environment where sudden, unexpected noise is acceptable. These assumptions are not only patronizing but also potentially disruptive or even dangerous. I've been in quiet offices, libraries, and even hospitals where an unexpected loud noise could be highly inappropriate or disturbing. Having individual volume controls for each audio source allows users to tailor their experience to their specific needs and environment. It's a matter of respect for the user's context and preferences.

Just my $0.02, but I think on/mute gets the job done.

I noticed these folks didn't bother with a volume slider on their embedded video: http://ycombinator.posterous.com/embark-nyc-is-the-1-mta-award-winning-best-ap ====== dtsingletary It's absolutely necessary; it should, in fact, be illegal to omit a volume control on any audio-playing control, embed, device, or otherwise. This is a bit of a tangent, but stay with me: Much like with playing audio without asking for an opt-in (PLAY button), not having a volume control makes assumptions: a) The computer's volume is at a sane level. b) The user is prepared for audio to suddenly blare out of their speakers or headphones at an unpredictable volume level. c) The user is in an environment where sudden, unexpected noise is acceptable. These assumptions are not only patronizing but also potentially disruptive or eveN dangerous. I've been in quiet offices, libraries, and even hospitals where an unexpected loud noise could be highly inappropriate or disturbing. Having individual volume controls for each audio source allows users to tailor their experience to their specific needs and environment. It's a matter of respect for the user's context and preferences.

base_text: Although an X-ray structure for the M1 matrix protein is available ([@bib23]), its ultrastructure remains controversial ([@bib14]). The RNP core of the virion is not included in our simulations, being replaced by a sphere of suitably restrained particles. The RPO shell does not interact directly with the lipid envelope in our model. In vivo, the inner leaflet of the envelope appears to be lined by the M1 matrix protein, which may influence lipid dynamics. Other approaches could include the use of mobile anions to model the RNA ([@bib32]).

Production

Oselvar has been developed with three production wells. Produced oil and gas is transported to the Ula field platform for processing via the Oselvar module. Once processed, the oil will be pumped via Norpipe system to Teesside, UK. Although production was scheduled to start in November 2011, production started on April 14, 2012. See also

Oselvar oil field

Oselvar () is an offshore oil field located southwest of Stavanger in the southern Norwegian section of North Sea, close to the British border. Oselvar was discovered in 1991. The field is also at distance from Ula field to which it is tied up to via subsea pipeline. Estimated reserves at Oselvar stand at 38 million barrels of oil and of natural gas.
base_text:
Oselvar oil field

Oselvar () is an offshore oil field located southwest of Stavanger in the southern Norwegian section of North Sea, close to the British border. Oselvar was discovered in 1991. The field is also at distance from Ula field to which it is tied up to via subsea pipeline. Estimated reserves at Oselvar stand at 38 million barrels of oil and of natural gas. Production began in 2007, and the field is operated by Equinor. The water depth at Oselvar is 280 metres, and it produces from a sandstone reservoir. The field’s production is challenging due to high pressure and low permeability. Oselvar has been subject to several modifications and enhanced oil recovery measures to maximise production and extend its lifespan. These have included water flooding and gas lift. The field's name originates from the Old Norse name Øseilvagr, meaning "bay with the sea eagle."
